page: Microsoft Azure D64pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dplsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores powered by the Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The system is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression tests scoring in the top 10% of tested servers. Memory subsystem performance is also highly rated, with cached and uncached read-only memory bandwidth, uncached write bandwidth, and PassMark memory scores all placing in the top 10%. Single-core performance, including stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core metrics, falls in the average tier.

The server presents a clear architectural tradeoff: while multi-threaded compute and memory bandwidth are highly efficient, the 2.0 GB of RAM per core represents a lower memory-to-CPU ratio compared to standard general-purpose instances. This low-memory profile, combined with the lack of local storage, positions the instance as a cost-efficient option for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive in-memory databases or local NVMe drives.

Given these characteristics, the D64pls_v6 is suited for multi-threaded CPU-bound tasks, software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and parallelized data processing workloads that fit within the 128.0 GB memory envelope.
description: Microsoft Azure D64pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Dplsv6 family, featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 2.0 GB per core memory-to-CPU ratio. It contains no local storage or GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks reveal top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and strong static web serving throughput, while single-core performance remains average. The low memory-to-CPU ratio represents a tradeoff, making it a cost-efficient option for compute-dense workloads rather than memory-heavy applications. It is suited for software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded parallel processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 128 GB of RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance for compute-dense workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine with 64 dedicated cores and 128 GB of RAM, optimized for multi-threaded compute workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered 64-core virtual machine offering high multi-threaded compute density and top-tier memory bandwidth with a low-memory footprint.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core
